title: mirabilis oligantha
intro: Mirabilis oligantha, commonly known as the "Mirabilis jalapa" or "Four O'Clock", is a flowering plant species native to the Americas. It has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and skin irritations. The therapeutic actions of Mirabilis oligantha include its ability to stimulate digestion, relieve respiratory issues, and reduce swelling, making it a popular remedy in herbal medicine. The bioactive constituents responsible for its medicinal properties include alkaloids, glycosides, and saponins, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities.
side_effects: Mirabilis oligantha can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and gastrointestinal issues. If ingested in large quantities, it may cause nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea due to its toxic compounds. Prolonged exposure to its sap may also lead to photosensitivity and increased risk of skin cancer. Furthermore, improper use of the plant may also cause hormonal imbalances and reproductive issues in some individuals.
